title: fraxinus japonica f. stenocarpa
intro: Fraxinus japonica f. stenocarpa, also known as Japanese ash or white ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and distinctive white bark. The bark and leaves of the tree have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have medicinal properties in treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The health benefits of Fraxinus japonica f. stenocarpa include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Fraxinus japonica f. stenocarpa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
